subject: Geography
overall_learning_objectives: Explain the hydrological cycle and why water is a renewable resource
Analyze the causes of water scarcity in different regions
Understand the role of multipurpose river projects in water management
Evaluate both benefits and environmental impacts of dams
Explore traditional and modern rainwater harvesting techniques
Study regional irrigation practices in India
Develop problem-solving skills related to water conservation

lesson_plan.title: Hydrological Cycle and Water Distribution
student_activities: Identify sources of freshwater in their locality

lesson_plan.title: Understanding Water Scarcity
student_activities: Analyze a case study of water shortage in a city

lesson_plan.title: Need for Water Conservation
student_activities: Prepare a list of water conservation practices

lesson_plan.title: Multipurpose River Projects
topics_covered: Examples like Bhakra Nangal and Hirakud
student_activities: Locate major dams on an India map
List different uses of multipurpose projects

lesson_plan.title: Impacts of Large Dams
student_activities: Create a table comparing advantages and disadvantages
Participate in debate on large dams

lesson_plan.title: Rainwater Harvesting Methods
student_activities: Draw a rooftop rainwater harvesting model
Research traditional water harvesting in India

lesson_plan.title: Traditional Irrigation Systems and Revision
student_activities: Explain bamboo drip irrigation using a diagram

teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyze real water issues in their community and connect them with the concepts of water scarcity and conservation.
